Record Class PlayerRepresentation

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Record
cz.cvut.fel.pjv.mosteji1.poker.client.gameRepresentation.PlayerRepresentation
Record Components:
name - the name of the player
avatarIndex - the index of the player's avatar
chips - the number of chips the player has
bet - the amount the player has bet
folded - indicates if the player has folded
isAllIn - indicates if the player is all-in
myHand - the player's hand of cards
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public record PlayerRepresentation(String name, int avatarIndex, int chips, int bet, boolean folded, boolean isAllIn, ArrayList<Card> myHand) extends Record implements Serializable
Represents a player in a representation of the poker game that is being sent over the server. Implements Serializable to allow serialization of player objects.
See Also:

  • Constructor Details

    • PlayerRepresentation

      public PlayerRepresentation(String name, int avatarIndex, int chips, int bet, boolean folded, boolean isAllIn, ArrayList<Card> myHand)
      Creates an instance of a PlayerRepresentation record class.
      Parameters:
      name - the value for the name record component
      avatarIndex - the value for the avatarIndex record component
      chips - the value for the chips record component
      bet - the value for the bet record component
      folded - the value for the folded record component
      isAllIn - the value for the isAllIn record component
      myHand - the value for the myHand record component

  • Method Details

    • toString

      public final String toString()
      Returns a string representation of this record class. The representation contains the name of the class, followed by the name and value of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      toString in class Record
      Returns:
      a string representation of this object

    • hashCode

      public final int hashCode()
      Returns a hash code value for this object. The value is derived from the hash code of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      hashCode in class Record
      Returns:
      a hash code value for this object

    • equals

      public final boolean equals(Object o)
      Indicates whether some other object is "equal to" this one. The objects are equal if the other object is of the same class and if all the record components are equal. Reference components are compared with Objects::equals(Object,Object); primitive components are compared with the compare method from their corresponding wrapper classes.
      Specified by:
      equals in class Record
      Parameters:
      o - the object with which to compare
      Returns:
      true if this object is the same as the o argument; false otherwise.
